frame: The Character Double Standard: Women with Male Connections are Loose, Men with Female Connections are Desirable
generated_comment:
Kamla: Aajkal ki ladkiyan pehle din se hi ladkon ke saath itni ghul-mil jaati hain, yeh theek baat nahin hai. Nayi naukri hai, apna kaam karo aur seedha ghar aao, har Rahul-Amit ke saath chai pe baithne se baad mein baatein banti hain.
Husband: Haan, samjhana padega.
Kamla: Pyaar se samjhao usko, ladki ko thoda seema mein rehna chahiye, warna log bhi galat samajhte hain aur aadat bhi kharab ho jaati hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it applies a gendered double standard, treating a young woman's comfort with male colleagues as improper and morally risky. It encodes casual social control and reputation-policing of women through notions of 'limits' and respectability.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 60. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 22 has just started her first job. She comes home talking about her male colleagues Ñ Rahul said this, Amit did that, they all went for chai after work. You are listening. You are not saying anything in the moment. But you are sitting with your husband that night and telling him what you have been hearing and what you think about a girl who is new to a job and already so comfortable with so many boys and what this kind of comfort leads to and what needs to be said to her.
